answer:
Allergy testing and treatment, including skin and blood tests, allergy shots, and serums, are covered when provided by a certified or licensed allergy specialist.

| Benefit | Service | In-Network | Out-of-Network | Limitations |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Allergy Testing And Treatment | Allergy Testing And Treatment | Deductible, then 20% coinsurance | Deductible, then 40% coinsurance | Data Not Found |
| Professional Visits and Services | Office and clinic visits | Kinwell Clinics: $0 copay per visit, deductible waived  All Other Non-Specialist: $25 copay per visit, deductible waived  All Other Specialist: $40 copay per visit, deductible waived | Deductible, then 40% coinsurance | You may have extra costs for other services like lab tests and facility charges. Also see Allergy Testing And Treatment and Therapeutic Injections. |
| Professional Visits and Services | Other professional services | Deductible, then 20% coinsurance | Deductible, then 40% coinsurance | You may have extra costs for other services like lab tests and facility charges. Also see Allergy Testing And Treatment and Therapeutic Injections. |

| Topic | Coverage Information |
| :--- | :--- |
| Allergy Testing And Treatment | Skin and blood tests used to diagnose what substances a person is allergic to, and treatment for allergies. Services must be provided by a certified or licensed allergy specialist. This benefit covers: • Testing • Allergy shots • Serums |
source: Premera Employees Health Plan – Standard PPO Retiree Plan
